info.parenttitle: A3.4 Introduction to electronic systems (HL only)

markdown:
## Input Devices


<definition term="Input">What is put into a system</definition>

1. Input devices **detect changes in the environment** and send signals into an electronic system. 
2. They act as the system’s “senses,” triggering a process or output.

### Types of Input Devices


1. **Analogue Input Devices**
  1. **Produce continuous signals** that vary over time.
  2. Examples: **Temperature sensors, light sensors**.
2. **Digital Input Devices**
  1. **Produce discrete signals**, often in the form of **binary data** (0s and 1s).
  2. Examples: **Switches, digital microphones**.

<callout type="note">**Analogue signals** are continuous and can take any value within a range, while digital signals are discrete and represent data in binary form.</callout>

#### Switches


1. **Purpose**: Turn a circuit **on or off** manually or mechanically.
2. **Functionality: **
  1. When a switch is **closed**, it **completes the circuit**, allowing current to flow.
  2. When **open**, it **breaks the circuit**, stopping the flow of current.
3. **Examples**: Push buttons, toggle switches, reed switches (activated by magnets).
4. **Signal Type**: **Digital** (just two states – ON/OFF).

![Types of switches (peerless electronics)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/405af64b38bdee07a40528ee59ecf8de9ea99753-1920x1200.png)

#### Sensors


<definition term="Sensor">An **electronic input device** that **detects or measures physical changes** in the environment, such as **light, temperature, or motion, **and converts them into **electrical signals**.</definition>

| Sensor Type | Detects | Example Use | Signal Type |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Light Sensor (LDR) | Light level | Auto night lights, phone brightness | Analogue |
| Temperature Sensor | Heat changes | Thermostats, smart fridges | Analogue |
| Humidity Sensor | Moisture in air | Weather stations, indoor air quality systems | Analogue |
| Sound Sensor | Volume or noise level | Voice-activated systems | Analogue |
| PIR Sensor | Motion detection | Security lights, alarms | Digital |

<callout type="example">An **LDR** in a streetlight system detects decreasing light levels at dusk, triggering the light to turn on.</callout>

![An LDR in a streetlight system detects decreasing light levels at dusk, triggering the light to turn on (ALION)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/e30493aec4b28bda6d96ec3d87b2370420958ded-2048x1365.png)

<callout type="note">**Humidity sensors** are crucial in agriculture, where maintaining optimal moisture levels can significantly impact crop yield.</callout>
